prompt.text: Which is the first step in the recommended approach to defining and analyzing the products?

options.text: Creating a product flow diagram
options.explain: Incorrect. This is done after the breakdown structure.

options.text: Creating a product breakdown structure
options.explain: Incorrect. This is the second step.

options.text: Writing a project product description
options.explain: Correct. This is the first step to understand the final deliverable.

options.text: Writing a product description
options.explain: Incorrect. These are created for individual components later.

prompt.text: Which of the following statements is a definition of customer quality expectations?

options.text: A summary of the quality activities planned for the project
options.explain: Incorrect. This describes a quality management approach.

options.text: A description of the measurable attributes of a product
options.explain: Incorrect. These are quality criteria.

options.text: A description of the quality expected of the project’s products
options.explain: Correct. Customer quality expectations define the acceptable quality level for the final product.

options.text: The measurable improvement resulting from an outcome
options.explain: Incorrect. This describes a benefit.

prompt.text: Who does the senior supplier represent when making decisions?

options.text: Those who will benefit from using the products
options.explain: Incorrect. This is the Senior User's domain.

options.text: Corporate, programme management, or the customer
options.explain: Incorrect. The Executive typically represents these interests on the board.

options.text: Those who ensure the project is following a cost-conscious approach
options.explain: Incorrect. The Executive ensures value for money.

options.text: Those who are responsible for the technical integrity of the product
options.explain: Correct. The Senior Supplier represents the interests of those designing, developing, and implementing the products.

prompt.text: Which of the following describes an advantage of applying the focus on products principle?

options.text: The project management method used is appropriate to the project.
options.explain: Incorrect. This refers to the 'Tailor to suit the project environment' principle.

options.text: Projects that can longer be justified are stopped.
options.explain: Incorrect. This is 'Continued business justification'.

options.text: Senior management’s time is not wasted managing the project on a day-to-day basis.
options.explain: Incorrect. This is 'Manage by exception'.

options.text: The project does no more work than necessary to deliver its products.
options.explain: Correct. Focusing on products ensures the scope is strictly limited to required deliverables.

prompt.text: Which product is used at the end of the project to assess how the project performed?

options.text: Project brief
options.explain: Incorrect. This is a pre-project document.

options.text: Project initiation documentation
options.explain: Correct. The PID contains the baseline against which final performance is assessed.

options.text: Project product description
options.explain: Incorrect. This is used to verify the final product, not overall project performance.

options.text: Project mandate
options.explain: Incorrect. This triggers the project.

prompt.text: Which of the following are objectives of the initiating a project process?

options.text: To ensure that there is common understanding of how the quality required will be achieved
options.explain: Correct. This is done by creating the Quality Management Approach.

options.text: To identify who needs information, in what format, and at what time
options.explain: Correct. This is done by creating the Communication Management Approach.

options.text: To ensure that all the necessary authorities exist for initiating the project
options.explain: Incorrect. This happens during Starting up a Project.

options.text: To plan the work required for project initiation
options.explain: Incorrect. The Initiation Stage Plan is created during Starting up a Project.

prompt.text: Which of the following is a purpose of the starting up a project process?

options.text: To establish solid foundations for the delivery of the project
options.explain: Incorrect. This is the purpose of Initiating a Project.

options.text: To assign and monitor work packages
options.explain: Incorrect. This is Controlling a Stage / Managing Product Delivery.

options.text: To enable the project board to be accountable for the project’s success
options.explain: Incorrect. This happens via Directing a Project.

options.text: To resource and allocate key roles and responsibilities
options.explain: Correct. Designing and appointing the project management team is a key activity here.

prompt.text: Which option represents a characteristic of projects?

options.text: Involves work with no defined end point
options.explain: Incorrect. Projects are temporary and have an end.

options.text: Involves work across multiple functional divisions
options.explain: Correct. Projects are cross-functional by nature.

options.text: Involves predictable, recurring work
options.explain: Incorrect. This is Business As Usual (BAU).

options.text: Involves work that brings little change to an organization
options.explain: Incorrect. Projects introduce change.

prompt.text: Which of the following options is a factor to consider when structuring the project into management stages?

options.text: The availability of the project board
options.explain: Incorrect. The board should be available for stage boundaries, but their calendar doesn't dictate the structure.

options.text: The estimating techniques used for planning
options.explain: Incorrect. Estimation is used within planning, not for defining stage boundaries.

options.text: The critical path of the project
options.explain: Incorrect. The critical path affects schedule, but management stages are based on commitment and risk.

options.text: Alignment with programme activities
options.explain: Correct. Stage boundaries should align with the overarching programme's decision points.

prompt.text: Which two management products does the business case theme require be produced and maintained?

options.text: Risk register
options.explain: Incorrect. This belongs to the Risk theme.

options.text: Communication management approach
options.explain: Incorrect. This belongs to the Organization theme.

options.text: Business case
options.explain: Correct. This justifies the project.

options.text: Benefits management approach
options.explain: Correct. This defines how benefits will be measured and realized.

prompt.text: Which of the following options does PRINCE2 recommend in the designing a plan step of the approach to planning?

options.text: Creating a product breakdown structure to help identify products
options.explain: Incorrect. This is part of 'Defining and analyzing the products'.

options.text: Identifying the types of resources required for each activity
options.explain: Incorrect. This is part of 'Preparing estimates'.

options.text: Identifying uncertainties inherent in the plan
options.explain: Incorrect. This is part of 'Analyzing the risks'.

options.text: Identify the number and length of management stages
options.explain: Correct. Designing the plan involves deciding on the appropriate number and duration of management stages.

prompt.text: Which process provides the base information needed to make a rational decision about commissioning a project?

options.text: Directing a project
options.explain: Incorrect. The board makes the decision here, but doesn't compile the information.

options.text: Starting up a project
options.explain: Correct. This process gathers the preliminary data (Project Brief) to decide if initiation is worthwhile.

options.text: Initiating a project
options.explain: Incorrect. Initiation happens after the project is commissioned.

options.text: Managing a stage boundary
options.explain: Incorrect. This is for continuation decisions between stages.

prompt.text: Which of the following levels of plans might be created by organizations that are not following the PRINCE2 method?

options.text: Project plan
options.explain: Incorrect. PRINCE2 requires this.

options.text: Stage plan
options.explain: Incorrect. PRINCE2 requires this for management control.

options.text: Team plan
options.explain: Correct. Team plans are optional in PRINCE2 and can be handled by standard team management tools outside the core framework.

options.text: Exception plan
options.explain: Incorrect. This is a specific PRINCE2 control mechanism.

prompt.text: Which of the following options represents the user interest on a project?

options.text: Someone who can assess and confirm the viability of the project approach
options.explain: Incorrect. This is typically the Executive or Supplier.

options.text: Someone who will represent those who maintain the project’s products in their operational life
options.explain: Correct. Operations and maintenance staff fall under the user perspective.

options.text: Someone who can represent those who will buy the project’s products once they have been developed
options.explain: Correct. Customers/buyers are considered users.

options.text: Someone who will be in charge of marketing the project’s products once they have been developed
options.explain: Incorrect. Marketing usually aligns with the Business perspective (Executive).

prompt.text: Which of the following statements apply to an exception plan?

options.text: The project board approves an exception plan that replaces a project plan.
options.explain: Incorrect. Corporate/Programme management approves project-level exception plans.

options.text: An exception plan may show actions required to recover from the effect of a forecast breach in project tolerance levels.
options.explain: Correct. It provides a new baseline for recovery.

options.text: Once approved, an exception plan could become the new baselined stage plan.
options.explain: Correct. It replaces the current stage plan.

options.text: An exception plan may show the actions required to recover from the effect of a forecast breach in work package tolerance levels.
options.explain: Incorrect. Work package tolerances are handled by the Project Manager without an Exception Plan.

prompt.text: Which of the following statements is true of project assurance?

options.text: Project assurance has three focuses aligned to the three interests of the project board.
options.explain: Correct. Assurance covers Business, User, and Supplier interests.

options.text: Project support can be assigned project assurance responsibilities.
options.explain: Incorrect. Assurance and Support must be kept separate to maintain independence.

options.text: The project manager can be assigned project assurance responsibilities
options.explain: Incorrect. The PM cannot audit themselves.

options.text: Project assurance creates the communication management approach.
options.explain: Incorrect. The Project Manager creates this.

prompt.text: Which of the following statements describes a minimum requirement for applying the quality theme?

options.text: To review the business justification when exceptions are raised
options.explain: Incorrect. This belongs to the Business Case theme.

options.text: To define how to identify ways of eliminating causes of unsatisfactory performance
options.explain: Correct. This refers to the implementation of lessons to improve quality.

options.text: To ensure that the project has at least two management stages
options.explain: Incorrect. This is a minimum requirement of the Organization/Plans themes.

options.text: To define the project’s approach to communicating and engaging with stakeholders
options.explain: Incorrect. This is the Organization theme.

prompt.text: Why might a separate person be appointed as a team manager?

options.text: A team has insufficient motivation due to micromanagement.
options.explain: Incorrect. This is a leadership issue, not a structural reason for the role.

options.text: A team is sited at a different geographic location from the project manager.
options.explain: Correct. Geographical separation requires local management.

options.text: A team is building products using a specialist skill.
options.explain: Correct. The PM may not have the technical knowledge to manage specialist work directly.

options.text: A team already has a project manager.
options.explain: Incorrect. Irrelevant to the PRINCE2 Team Manager role designation.

prompt.text: In which of the following steps in the approach to planning does PRINCE2 recommend using the product-based planning approach?

options.text: Designing the plan
options.explain: Incorrect. This is about deciding format and presentation.

options.text: Defining and analyzing the products
options.explain: Correct. Product-based planning defines the outputs before scheduling activities.

options.text: Identifying activities and dependencies
options.explain: Incorrect. This comes after product definition.

options.text: Preparing estimates
options.explain: Incorrect. This relies on the identified activities.

prompt.text: Which of these options is a minimum requirement for managing business justification for the project?

options.text: To split the project into at least two management stages
options.explain: Incorrect. This is related to the Plans and Organization themes.

options.text: To record all quality activities carried out during the project
options.explain: Incorrect. This is the Quality theme.

options.text: To define a method for baselining products
options.explain: Incorrect. This is Configuration Management / Change theme.

options.text: To identify actions to ensure that the project’s outcomes are achieved
options.explain: Correct. This ensures benefits are realized, validating the business case.

prompt.text: Which principle addresses the problem that planning beyond a certain planning horizon is difficult?

options.text: Continued business justification
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Manage by stages
options.explain: Correct. By breaking the project into stages, detailed planning is only done for the immediate next stage.

options.text: Manage by exception
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Tailor to suit the project environment
options.explain: Incorrect.

prompt.text: Which of the following are purposes of the plans theme?

options.text: To define the project’s structure of accountability and responsibility
options.explain: Incorrect. This is the Organization theme.

options.text: To establish whether the targets for time and cost are achievable
options.explain: Correct. Planning provides the timeline and budget.

options.text: To provide a baseline against which progress can be measured
options.explain: Correct. Plans act as a control baseline.

options.text: To establish mechanisms to judge whether the project is desirable, viable, and achievable
options.explain: Incorrect. This is the Business Case theme.

prompt.text: Which is a purpose of the quality register?

options.text: To define the project’s approach to quality control
options.explain: Incorrect. This is the Quality Management Approach.

options.text: To specify a product’s quality criteria
options.explain: Incorrect. This is done in the Product Description.

options.text: To act as a pointer to quality records associated with a product
options.explain: Correct. It summarizes all planned and completed quality activities.

options.text: To specify a product’s quality methods
options.explain: Incorrect. Also done in the Product Description.

prompt.text: Which role is responsible for ensuring that the benefits forecast in the business case represent value for money and align with the organization’s objectives?

options.text: Executive
options.explain: Correct. The Executive secures funding and is ultimately responsible for the Business Case.

options.text: Project manager
options.explain: Incorrect. The PM manages the project day-to-day.

options.text: Project support
options.explain: Incorrect. Support provides administrative help.

options.text: Senior user
options.explain: Incorrect. The Senior User specifies benefits but doesn't guarantee overall value for money.

prompt.text: Which of the following is one of the benefits of product-based planning?

options.text: It helps to define products that are in and out of scope of the plan.
options.explain: Correct. Clearly identifying all products explicitly outlines the project's scope boundary.

options.text: It helps to identify the shortest possible completion time of the project.
options.explain: Incorrect. Scheduling/Critical path analysis does this.

options.text: It helps to identify how the performance of the project’s products will be reviewed.
options.explain: Incorrect. Quality methods handle this.

options.text: It helps to define techniques for estimating throughout a project.
options.explain: Incorrect. Estimation is a separate step.

prompt.text: Identify the missing word(s) in the following sentence: [?] is achieved by implementing, monitoring, and recording the quality methods of the project.

options.text: Quality planning
options.explain: Incorrect. Planning is done beforehand.

options.text: Quality control
options.explain: Correct. Control involves executing the methods and recording results.

options.text: Quality assurance
options.explain: Incorrect. Assurance is independent of the project.

options.text: Project assurance
options.explain: Incorrect. Assurance verifies processes, not the direct implementation of quality methods.

prompt.text: Which of these options is a minimum requirement for managing the plans of the project?

options.text: To split the project into at least two management stages
options.explain: Correct. The project must have at least an initiation stage and one delivery stage.

options.text: To record all quality activities carried out during the project
options.explain: Incorrect. Quality theme requirement.

options.text: To define a method for baselining products
options.explain: Incorrect. Change theme requirement.

options.text: To identify actions to ensure that the project’s outcomes are achieved
options.explain: Incorrect. Business Case requirement.

prompt.text: Which of the following might be used by the commissioning organization to provide information for the project mandate?

options.text: A feasibility study
options.explain: Correct. A pre-project study can trigger a mandate.

options.text: A request for proposal
options.explain: Correct. RFPs often serve as triggers for projects.

options.text: A project brief
options.explain: Incorrect. The brief is created after the mandate.

options.text: A project initiation documentation
options.explain: Incorrect. PID is created even later.

prompt.text: Which of the following statements are true of both project assurance and quality assurance?

options.text: They provide assurance that the project is being run to corporate standards.
options.explain: Correct. Both ensure compliance with standards.

options.text: They are independent of the managing level of the project management team.
options.explain: Correct. Neither role reports directly to the Project Manager.

options.text: The role is carried out on behalf of the business, user, and supplier perspectives of the project board.
options.explain: Incorrect. Quality Assurance represents corporate/programme, not specific board perspectives.

options.text: They are independent of the directing level of the project management team.
options.explain: Incorrect. Project Assurance is part of the directing level.

prompt.text: Who is responsible for specifying the benefits and subsequently realizing the benefits by using the project’s product?

options.text: Project manager
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Executive
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Senior supplier
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Senior user
options.explain: Correct. The Senior User ensures the desired benefits are achieved post-project.

prompt.text: Which PRINCE2 role is responsible for securing the funding for the project?

options.text: Corporate, programme management, or the customer
options.explain: Incorrect. They provide funding, but the Executive secures it for the specific project.

options.text: Executive
options.explain: Correct. Securing and managing the budget is the Executive's responsibility.

options.text: Business sponsor
options.explain: Incorrect. Not a standard PRINCE2 role, though the Executive acts as one.

options.text: Project manager
options.explain: Incorrect.

prompt.text: Which process provides information that the project board uses when deciding whether to initiate the project?

options.text: Starting up a project
options.explain: Correct. This process produces the Project Brief for the initiation decision.

options.text: Directing a project
options.explain: Incorrect. This is where the decision is made, not where the information is generated.

options.text: Initiating a project
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Managing a stage boundary
options.explain: Incorrect.

prompt.text: In a small project, it may be appropriate for the project manager to also carry out the responsibilities of which of the following roles?

options.text: Executive
options.explain: Incorrect. Conflict of interest.

options.text: Project assurance
options.explain: Incorrect. The PM cannot audit themselves.

options.text: Project support
options.explain: Correct. If the project is small, the PM can handle administrative support tasks.

options.text: Quality assurance
options.explain: Incorrect. Must be independent.

prompt.text: Which PRINCE2 management product helps establish a controlled flow of information between all the project stakeholders?

options.text: Project brief
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Risk management approach
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Communication management approach
options.explain: Correct. It defines how, when, and with whom to communicate.

options.text: Project approach
options.explain: Incorrect.

prompt.text: Which of the following is a purpose of the initiating a project process?

options.text: To answer the question, “Do we have a viable and worthwhile project?”
options.explain: Incorrect. This is Starting up a Project.

options.text: To prevent poorly conceived projects from being initiated
options.explain: Incorrect. This is the goal of the pre-project stage.

options.text: To make key decisions in and exercise overall control over the project
options.explain: Incorrect. This is Directing a Project.

options.text: To enable the commissioning organization to understand the work that needs to be done to deliver the project’s products
options.explain: Correct. Creating the PID ensures common understanding of the required work.

prompt.text: Which of the following is a purpose of the business case theme?

options.text: To help establish whether the project is still viable when making key decisions
options.explain: Correct. The Business Case drives decision-making to ensure continued viability.

options.text: To understand whether the outputs from the project are fit for purpose
options.explain: Incorrect. Quality theme.

options.text: To establish a structure of accountability for the project
options.explain: Incorrect. Organization theme.

options.text: To assess and control uncertainty throughout the project
options.explain: Incorrect. Risk theme.

prompt.text: Which of the following is an example of the business case being developed?

options.text: The executive drafting the outline business case
options.explain: Correct. The outline business case is developed during Starting up a Project.

options.text: The project board reviewing the justification for the project
options.explain: Incorrect. This is reviewing, not developing.

options.text: The senior user providing a statement of the achievement of benefits during a benefit review
options.explain: Incorrect. This is benefits realization.

options.text: The project manager updating the business case at the end of a stage
options.explain: Incorrect. This is maintaining, not the initial development.

prompt.text: Which process ensures that the project remains aligned to the commissioning organization’s strategy?

options.text: Starting up a project
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Directing a project
options.explain: Correct. The Project Board maintains strategic alignment through direction and approvals.

options.text: Initiating a project
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Controlling a stage
options.explain: Incorrect.

prompt.text: Which level of management would authorize any deviation that exceeds stage tolerances?

options.text: Corporate, programme management, or the customer
options.explain: Incorrect. They handle project-level tolerances.

options.text: Directing
options.explain: Correct. The Project Board (Directing level) handles stage-level tolerance deviations.

options.text: Managing
options.explain: Incorrect. The PM is the one escalating the issue.

options.text: Delivering
options.explain: Incorrect. Team managers handle work package tolerances.

prompt.text: Which of the following is a purpose of the project product description?

options.text: To gain agreement from the user on the project’s scope and requirements
options.explain: Correct. It defines what the final output should be, establishing scope.

options.text: To define how the project will be managed in order to deliver products that are fit for their purpose
options.explain: Incorrect. This is the Quality Management Approach.

options.text: To define how the project will control uncertainty
options.explain: Incorrect. Risk Management Approach.

options.text: To define how the project will control proposed modifications to the project’s products
options.explain: Incorrect. Change Control Approach.

prompt.text: Delegating authority from one management level to the next supports which principle?

options.text: Continued business justification
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Learn from experience
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Manage by stages
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Manage by exception
options.explain: Correct. Tolerances are set, allowing the lower level to operate independently until a limit is breached.

prompt.text: Which of the following statement describes a minimum requirement for applying the quality theme?

options.text: Using the quality review technique to inspect the project’s products
options.explain: Incorrect. This specific technique is recommended, not mandatory.

options.text: Using quality inspections to assess a product’s fitness for purpose
options.explain: Incorrect. The method can vary.

options.text: Using both in-process and appraisal quality methods
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Maintaining records of quality activities
options.explain: Correct. Auditable records (like a Quality Register) must be kept.

prompt.text: Which of the following is a quality planning activity?

options.text: Creating product descriptions to understand what is required of a product
options.explain: Correct. Defining requirements happens in planning.

options.text: Scheduling the development activities of a product so that it will match its product description
options.explain: Incorrect. This is part of the Plans theme.

options.text: Carrying out a quality inspection on a completed product
options.explain: Incorrect. This is quality control.

options.text: Defining the quality methods that will be used to check that a product conforms to its quality criteria
options.explain: Correct. Selecting the methods is a planning step.

prompt.text: Which of the following options represents an example of an output?

options.text: Benefits management approach
options.explain: Incorrect. This is a management product.

options.text: New computer software
options.explain: Correct. An output is the tangible or intangible specialist product created by the project.

options.text: Quicker processing of invoices
options.explain: Incorrect. This is an outcome.

options.text: A 20-percent increase in productivity
options.explain: Incorrect. This is a benefit.

prompt.text: Which two statements about tailoring are correct?

options.text: PRINCE2 projects should use the recommended PRINCE2 techniques.
options.explain: Incorrect. Techniques can be replaced by organizational equivalents.

options.text: Management products might be split into many data sources.
options.explain: Correct. Products don't have to be Word documents; they can be Jira boards, databases, etc.

options.text: PRINCE2 projects can comprise as many management stages as are necessary.
options.explain: Correct. As long as there are at least two.

options.text: Principles that are not relevant to the project can be excluded.
options.explain: Incorrect. All principles must be applied.

prompt.text: At what time in the project is the business case reviewed and maybe updated?

options.text: When refining the business case during the initiation stage
options.explain: Correct. The outline is expanded into a detailed business case here.

options.text: When creating the project mandate
options.explain: Incorrect. The mandate is just the trigger.

options.text: At the end of each management stage
options.explain: Correct. During Managing a Stage Boundary, the PM updates the business case for board review.

options.text: When delivering specialist products
options.explain: Incorrect. This happens during execution.

prompt.text: Which of the following is an objective of the starting up a project process?

options.text: To evaluate the various ways the project can be delivered
options.explain: Correct. Identifying the project approach involves considering different delivery options.

options.text: To decide whether to appoint a change authority
options.explain: Incorrect. This is done during Initiation.

options.text: To ensure that there is common understanding of how the quality required will be achieved
options.explain: Incorrect. Initiation process objective.

options.text: To prepare a plan to show how the achievement of the project’s benefits will be measured
options.explain: Incorrect. Creating the Benefits Management Approach happens in Initiation.

prompt.text: Which principle is being applied when quality criteria are specified in product descriptions?

options.text: Learn from experience
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Continued business justification
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Focus on products
options.explain: Correct. Defining quality criteria per product embodies the product-focused approach.

options.text: Manage by exception
options.explain: Incorrect.

prompt.text: What is one of the benefits provided by PRINCE2?

options.text: Sets out how to manage projects in particular industries
options.explain: Incorrect. It's industry-agnostic.

options.text: Provides motivational techniques
options.explain: Incorrect. It doesn't cover leadership/soft skills.

options.text: Provides a common project vocabulary
options.explain: Correct. A standard vocabulary streamlines communication.

options.text: Shows how to manage business as usual
options.explain: Incorrect. It is specifically for project work.

prompt.text: Which of the following is a purpose of the organization theme?

options.text: To manage uncertainty within the project
options.explain: Incorrect. Risk theme.

options.text: To define the means of delivering the products
options.explain: Incorrect. Project Approach.

options.text: To establish an effective approach for communication
options.explain: Correct. The Organization theme includes the Communication Management Approach.

options.text: To define how the project will verify that products are fit for purpose
options.explain: Incorrect. Quality theme.

prompt.text: During which of the following processes is the outline business case refined into the detailed business case?

options.text: Starting up a project
options.explain: Incorrect. The outline is created here.

options.text: Initiating a project
options.explain: Correct. The PM develops the detailed business case here with more accurate estimates.

options.text: Managing a stage boundary
options.explain: Incorrect. It's updated, not initially detailed, here.

options.text: Directing a project
options.explain: Incorrect.

prompt.text: Which of the following describes how to manage quality throughout a project?

options.text: Project product description
options.explain: Incorrect. Defines overall requirements.

options.text: Quality management approach
options.explain: Correct. This document describes the strategies, procedures, and techniques for quality.

options.text: Product description
options.explain: Incorrect. Defines criteria for a specific product.

options.text: Quality register
options.explain: Incorrect. This is a log, not a procedural guide.

prompt.text: Which aspect of project performance that needs to be managed helps set the objectives for the expected return on the project?

options.text: Costs
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Risks
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Benefits
options.explain: Correct. Benefits represent the measurable return on investment.

options.text: Scope
options.explain: Incorrect.

prompt.text: Which of the following is a purpose of the benefits management approach?

options.text: To describe how and when the performance of the project’s products can be measured
options.explain: Correct. It sets the baseline and timeline for post-project benefit realization checks.

options.text: To document the justification for the project
options.explain: Incorrect. This is the Business Case.

options.text: To define the project
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: To pass on details of unfinished work and ongoing risks
options.explain: Incorrect. Follow-on action recommendations.

prompt.text: Which of the following is an objective of the starting up a project process?

options.text: To plan the management activities needed to ensure that the project outcomes are achieved
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: To describe how to tailor the commissioning organization’s project management method
options.explain: Incorrect. Tailoring is documented during Initiation.

options.text: To create a business justification for the initiation of the project
options.explain: Correct. Creating the Outline Business Case secures approval for the initiation stage.

options.text: To provide management direction and control throughout the project
options.explain: Incorrect. Directing a project.

prompt.text: How is the principle of continued business justification applied?

options.text: By re-evaluating the reason for the project throughout the life of the project
options.explain: Correct. Justification is not a one-time event; it must remain valid until closure.

options.text: By seeking opportunities to implement improvements throughout the life of the project
options.explain: Incorrect. Learn from experience.

options.text: By using product description to provide clarity around user’s requirements
options.explain: Incorrect. Focus on products.

options.text: By ensuring that there are review and decision points throughout the project
options.explain: Incorrect. Manage by stages.

prompt.text: Which is a minimum requirement for applying the organization theme?

options.text: To establish both a customer and supplier project board
options.explain: Incorrect. One integrated board is required.

options.text: To appoint the design authority to the role of change authority when the project is part of a programme
options.explain: Incorrect. Too specific, not a minimum requirement.

options.text: To appoint at least one person to carry out each of the PRINCE2 roles
options.explain: Incorrect. Roles can be combined depending on project size.

options.text: To ensure that all the responsibilities in the PRINCE2 role descriptions are fulfilled
options.explain: Correct. Responsibilities must be covered, even if roles are merged.

prompt.text: How does the plans theme help to implement the manage by exception principle?

options.text: By stating that all plans should contain a lessons incorporated section
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: By providing stage plans to help the project manager manage the day-to-day aspects of a stage
options.explain: Incorrect. Stage control, not exception.

options.text: By stating that all plans should contain a tolerance section
options.explain: Correct. Establishing tolerances creates limits; breaching them triggers an exception.

options.text: By providing a product-based planning approach
options.explain: Incorrect. Product focus.

prompt.text: Which principle ensures that the range of stakeholder perspectives is represented within the project management team?

options.text: Defined roles and responsibilities
options.explain: Correct. This principle ensures business, user, and supplier interests are covered.

options.text: Continued business justification
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Learn from experience
options.explain: Incorrect.

options.text: Tailor to suit the project environment
options.explain: Incorrect.

prompt.text: Which of the integrated elements of PRINCE2 provides a stepwise progression through the project life cycle?

options.text: Principles
options.explain: Incorrect. These are the guiding obligations.

options.text: Themes
options.explain: Incorrect. These are aspects that must be addressed continually.

options.text: Processes
options.explain: Correct. The 7 processes define the chronological flow of activities.

options.text: Tailoring to the project environment
options.explain: Incorrect. This adapts the method.
